draft.introduction: Rabia al-Adawiyya of Basra (d. 801) was the first great female Sufi saint and the pioneer of the doctrine of pure, disinterested love of God. Her prayers survive through later biographical collections.

translation.text: O God, if I worship You for fear of Hell, burn me in Hell. If I worship You in hope of Paradise, exclude me from Paradise. But if I worship You for Your own sake alone, do not withhold from me Your everlasting beauty.

translator_notes.note: This prayer became the defining statement of Sufi theology's highest aspiration: love of God entirely free from self-interest. It directly challenged the transactional model of worship that dominated popular piety. Later theologians debated whether such disinterested love was even possible for finite beings.
